description: Vietnam's pulsing economic engine — a city that moves at full throttle day and night, with some of Southeast Asia's best street food, a gripping War Remnants Museum, colonial French architecture, and a rooftop bar scene that makes you forget the motorbike chaos below.

neighborhoodRecs: District 1 (Ben Thanh area) — Tourist centre, best rooftop bars, War Remnants Museum, walking distance to most sights.
Thảo Điền (District 2) — Expat hub, excellent international restaurants, quieter, across the river from D1.
Bình Thạnh — Local neighbourhood with fantastic street food, Saigon Zoo, more residential feel.
District 3 — Trendy Vietnamese cafes, local food scene, less touristy than D1, good mid-range accommodation.
areasToAvoid: Bùi Viện Street (backpacker street) late at night — bag snatching and drink spiking have been reported.
Hailing motorbike taxis on the street — use the Grab app only for motorbike (GrabBike) and car rides.
Leaving valuables visible in outdoor café seating — drive-by bag snatches by motorbike are a real risk.
scamWarnings: Cyclo (pedicab) price agreement: always agree on the full price for the round trip before you start. Drivers will claim you agreed to $20/hour once you're back.
Fake lottery ticket sellers approach tourists near Ben Thanh Market — it's a distraction pickpocket technique.
Dollar exchange rates: hotels and airports offer poor rates. Vietcombank ATMs give good rates. ₫500,000 notes look similar to ₫20,000 — count carefully.
Taxi overcharging: only use Vinasun (green) or Mailinh (green) metered taxis, or Grab app. Fake 'Vinasun' taxis with altered meters are common.

airportTransfer.cheapest: Bus 109 from Tan Son Nhat airport to Ben Thanh Market — ₫20,000 (~$1), takes 40–60 min depending on traffic.
airportTransfer.fastest: Grab car from SGN — ₫120,000–200,000 (~$5–8) to District 1, takes 20–30 min. Book inside arrivals hall.
airportTransfer.recommended: Grab app is the safest, most transparent option — price shown upfront, no negotiation, metered.
airportTransfer.taxiEstimate: Vinasun or Mailinh metered taxi: ₫150,000–250,000 to District 1. Walk to the official taxi rank, ignore all touts.

tippingEtiquette: Tipping is not traditional in Vietnam but increasingly expected in tourist areas. ₫20,000–50,000 at restaurants.
Massage tip: ₫50,000–100,000 for a 60-minute massage is generous.
Vietnam is two seasons: dry season (Dec–Apr) is when you want to visit HCMC.
Motorbike traffic: never step into traffic expecting it to stop — watch how locals cross roads (slow, steady, predictable pace) and copy them.
The Vietnamese dong (₫) has 6 zeros — always count zeros carefully when paying.

dailyCostBreakdown.meals: Bánh mì street sandwich ₫30,000–50,000 | Phở bowl ₫50,000–80,000 | Restaurant dinner ₫150,000–400,000
dailyCostBreakdown.activities: War Remnants Museum ₫40,000 | Cu Chi Tunnels tour ₫250,000–400,000 | Cooking class ₫600,000–1,000,000

packingTips: Lightweight clothing only — HCMC is 28–32°C year-round with high humidity.
Rain jacket for May–October — daily afternoon downpours are brief but heavy.
Stomach preparation — Vietnamese street food is outstanding but the bacteria are different. Carry Imodium.
Grab app pre-installed — do this before you land. It works the moment you connect to airport WiFi.
premium.bookingStrategy: December–March is dry season and the best time to visit. Tết (Vietnamese New Year, late January/February) shuts the city down for 3–5 days — avoid if you want full access to restaurants and attractions.
localTips: Bánh mì from a street cart at 7am is one of Asia's great food experiences. ₫30,000–50,000 for the most flavourful sandwich you'll eat.
Bùi Viện Street is touristy but the beer is cheap (₫15,000 for a bia hơi draught) and the people-watching is excellent.
War Remnants Museum is essential but heavy — give yourself 2 hours and don't rush it. One of the most affecting museums in Asia.
The rooftop bar at Chill Skybar (District 1) has unbeatable Saigon cityscape views at sunset.
Cu Chi Tunnels: go on a weekday morning for a 90-min bus + tunnel experience — the crowds on weekends make it less impactful.
premium.bestTimeToVisit: December–February: dry, slightly cooler (27°C vs 32°C), manageable humidity.
dayTrips: Cu Chi Tunnels — 40 km northwest, the Viet Cong tunnel network used during the war, extraordinary engineering feat, 90 min by bus.
Mekong Delta — 90 min south, boat trips through river channels, floating markets, coconut candy factories, lush delta landscape.
Vũng Tàu — 2 hours by speedboat, seaside resort city, beaches (nothing special), good seafood, escape from city heat.
visaInfo: E-visa required for most nationalities — apply at evisa.xuatnhapcanh.gov.vn ($25 USD, 90-day single or multiple entry; processed within 3 business days)
Citizens of Germany, France, Italy, Spain, UK, and certain others receive a 45-day visa exemption — verify your nationality at the Vietnam MFA website
HCMC (formerly Saigon) is served by Tan Son Nhat Airport (SGN) — the busiest airport in Vietnam; e-visa arrivals are processed at the dedicated e-visa counter
Visa on arrival is available but requires advance arrangement; e-visa is far simpler for most nationalities
Passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond your planned departure from Vietnam
currencyTips: Currency: Vietnamese Dong (₫ / VND). The zero count on VND notes can be confusing — 500,000 VND is about $20 USD; 50,000 VND is about $2
Cash dominates in HCMC outside of shopping malls and international hotels — carry sufficient VND for street food, markets, and xe om rides
Best exchange: Vietcombank and Techcombank offer competitive rates in the city centre; the Ben Thanh area has reputable licensed exchange offices
ATMs: Vietcombank and ANZ ATMs work reliably with foreign cards; the standard foreign ATM fee is 40,000–85,000 VND per withdrawal
Grab (ride-hailing app) is essential for getting around HCMC — accepts cards and far more reliable than street taxis for foreigners
premium.simCard: Viettel and Mobifone both have strong 4G coverage in Ho Chi Minh City. SIMs are sold at Tan Son Nhat Airport (SGN) arrivals in the Viettel and Mobifone kiosks. A 30-day tourist SIM with 30–60 GB costs 100,000–200,000 VND. eSIM via Airalo is available for Vietnam and useful if you want connectivity immediately on landing.
